branch.cmd: git checkout -b epic/${EPIC_ID} || git checkout epic/${EPIC_ID}

commit.cmd: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(${TASK_ID}): task complete' --allow-empty

stories.title: Initialize project
acceptance_criteria: package.json exists with dependencies: express, better-sqlite3
package.json has devDependencies: vitest, supertest
npm install completes without errors
Express server in src/app.js exports the app and listens on port 3000 (or PORT env var)

SQLite database is initialized with a 'notes' table: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT), title (TEXT NOT NULL), body (TEXT DEFAULT ''), created_at (DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), updated_at (DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
npm test runs vitest and exits 0 (at least one placeholder test)

stories.title: POST /notes (TDD)
acceptance_criteria: Test: POST /notes with {title: 'My Note', body: 'Content'} returns 201 with JSON containing id (number), title, body, created_at, updated_at
Test: POST /notes with empty title returns 400 with {error: 'title is required'}
Test: POST /notes with title only (no body) returns 201 with body as empty string
Implementation makes all tests pass
npm test passes

stories.title: GET /notes + GET /notes/:id (TDD)
acceptance_criteria: Test: GET /notes on empty database returns 200 with []
Test: GET /notes after creating 2 notes returns 200 with array of length 2
Test: GET /notes returns notes sorted by created_at descending (newest first)
Test: GET /notes/:id with valid ID returns 200 with the note object
Test: GET /notes/:id with non-existent ID returns 404 with {error: 'note not found'}
Implementation makes all tests pass
npm test passes

stories.title: PUT /notes/:id + DELETE /notes/:id (TDD)
acceptance_criteria: Test: PUT /notes/:id with {title: 'Updated', body: 'New body'} returns 200 with updated note
Test: PUT /notes/:id updates the updated_at timestamp
Test: PUT /notes/:id with non-existent ID returns 404
Test: DELETE /notes/:id with valid ID returns 204 with no body
Test: DELETE /notes/:id with non-existent ID returns 404
Test: GET /notes/:id after DELETE returns 404 (note is actually removed)
Implementation makes all tests pass
npm test passes

stories.title: Search notes GET /notes?q=term (TDD)
acceptance_criteria: Test: GET /notes?q=hello with a note titled 'Hello World' returns that note in results
Test: GET /notes?q=content with a note body containing 'Some content here' returns that note
Test: GET /notes?q=nonexistent returns 200 with []
Test: GET /notes without q parameter returns all notes (backwards compatible)
Search is case-insensitive
Implementation uses SQLite LIKE for title and body columns
npm test passes

stories.title: Frontend: list notes page
acceptance_criteria: public/index.html exists and is served by Express at GET /
Page fetches GET /notes on load and renders a list of notes
Each note in the list shows: title, truncated body (max 100 characters with '...' if longer), and formatted date
When no notes exist, the page shows a 'No notes yet' message
Page has clean, readable CSS (no external CSS frameworks)
Opening the page in a browser shows no console errors

stories.title: Frontend: create and edit notes
acceptance_criteria: A form with a title input and a body textarea is visible on the page
Submitting the form with a title creates a new note via POST /notes and refreshes the list
Submitting with an empty title shows an error message (does not call the API)
Clicking a note in the list populates the form with that note's title and body for editing
Submitting while editing calls PUT /notes/:id and refreshes the list
A 'Cancel' button clears the form and exits edit mode
After creating or editing, the form is cleared

stories.title: Frontend: delete and search
acceptance_criteria: Each note in the list has a 'Delete' button
Clicking Delete shows a window.confirm prompt; confirming deletes via DELETE /notes/:id and refreshes the list
Clicking Delete and cancelling the confirm does nothing
A search input field is visible at the top of the notes list
Typing in the search input calls GET /notes?q=term and updates the displayed list
Search requests are debounced at 300ms (no API call until 300ms after last keystroke)
Clearing the search input shows all notes again
